3650	  is proper (\cref{lem:isProper_mathlib}). Since properness is stable under base
3651	  change (\cref{lem:isProper_mathlib}), this condition is preserved by pull-back
3652	  along any \(S'\to S\), which is exactly what the well-definedness of the Quot
3653	  functor's pullback action requires.
3654	\end{definition}
3655	
3656	\begin{definition}
3657	
3658	\leanok
3659	[Locally free of rank \(d\)]
3660	  \label{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
3661	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank}
3662	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises (2)" and
3663	  % "Grassmannian of a vector bundle" (read from
3664	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L562-L564
3665	  % + L584-L585).
3666	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3667	  % "$\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
3668	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
3669	  %  [...]
3670	  %  the set of all equivalence classes $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients
3671	  %  $q: E_T \to \F$ where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$,"
3672	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3673	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\) and let
3674	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). We say \(\mathcal{M}\) is \emph{locally free of rank
3675	  \(d\)} when there exists an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) together with
3676	  isomorphisms \(\mathcal{M}|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\oplus d}\) for
3677	  each \(i\).
3678	
3679	  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not provide this predicate.
3680	\end{definition}
3681	
3682	\section{The Quot functor}
3683	\label{sec:quot_functor}
3684	
3685	\begin{definition}\leanok
3686	[Quot functor]
3687	  \label{def:quot_functor}
3688	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor}
3689	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:has_proper_support, def:coherent_sheaf_flat,
3690	    lem:pullback_finite_presentation, lem:coherent_flat_base_change,
3691	    lem:proper_support_base_change, lem:hilbert_fibre_base_change}
3692	  % NOTE (run 0010 T12): the Lean definition is now REAL
3693	  % (AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean): value at T = Setoid quotient of
3694	  % `Scheme.QuotFamily` (finitely presented + flat + proper support + eventual-match
3695	  % Hilbert condition), morphism action = pullback along `quotBaseMap`.  The
3696	  % "coherent" clause is encoded as finite presentation
3697	  % (`SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation`), which over a locally noetherian base is
3698	  % coherence; condition (3) is encoded by eventual agreement of the graded Hilbert
3699	  % function with \(\Phi\) (exactly "the Hilbert polynomial of the fibre is
3700	  % \(\Phi\)" per def:hilbert_polynomial, excluding the junk-value coincidence).
3701	  % The functor is Type-((u+1))-valued (sheaves of modules are large); the
3702	  % representability statements are unaffected (RepresentableBy is
3703	  % universe-polymorphic).
3704	
3705	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) and
3706	  % \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\)" (read from
3707	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L394-L433
3708	  % + L481-L498).
3709	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3710	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(X\to S\) be a finite type
3711	  %  scheme over it. Let \(E\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X\).
3712	  %  Let \(Sch_S\) denote the category of all locally noetherian schemes
3713	  %  over \(S\). For any \(T\to S\) in \(Sch_S\), a \textbf{family of quotients of \(E\)
3714	  %  parametrised by \(T\)} will mean
3715	  %  a pair \((\F,q)\) consisting of
3716	  %
3717	  %  (i) a coherent sheaf \(\F\) on \(X_T = X\times_ST\)
3718	  %  such that the schematic support of \(\F\) is proper over \(T\)
3719	  %  and \(\F\) is flat over \(T\), together with
3720	  %
3721	  %  (ii) a surjective \({\mathcal O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism of sheaves
3722	  %  \(q:E_T  \to \F\) where \(E_T\) is the pull-back of \(E\) under
3723	  %  the projection \(X_T \to X\).
3724	  %
3725	  %  Two such families \((\F,q)\) and \((\F,q)\) parametrised by \(T\) will
3726	  %  be regarded as equivalent if \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\)),
3727	  %  and \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) will denote
3728	  %  an equivalence class. Then as properness and flatness are preserved
3729	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,
3730	  %  the pull-back of \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) under an \(S\)-morphism \(T'\to T\) is
3731	  %  well-defined, which gives
3732	  %  a set-valued contravariant functor
3733	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S} : Sch_S \to Sets\) under which
3734	  %  \(\)T\mapsto  \{\mbox{ All } \langle \F, q \rangle \mbox{ parametrised by }T\}\(\)
3735	  %  [...]
3736	  %  This shows that the functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\) naturally decomposes
3737	  %  as a co-product
3738	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\(\)
3739	  %  where for any polynomial \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\), the functor
3740	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) associates
3741	  %  to any \(T\) the set of all equivalence classes of
3742	  %  families \(\langle \F,q \rangle\) such that at each \(t\in T\) the
3743	  %  Hilbert polynomial of the restriction \(\F_t\), calculated
3744	  %  using the pull-back of \(L\), is \({\Phi}\)."
3745	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3746	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
3747	  \(L\) a line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\), and
3748	  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
3749	  polynomial \(\Phi\)}
3750	  \[
3751	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
3752	  \]
3753	  sends an \(S\)-scheme \(T \to S\) to the set of equivalence classes
3754	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
3755	  \begin{enumerate}
3756	    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
3757	      schematic support is proper over \(T\) (\cref{def:has_proper_support}) and
3758	      which is flat over \(T\);
3759	    \item \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is a surjective
3760	      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
3761	      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);
3762	    \item for every \(t \in T\) the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}\)
3763	      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
3764	      \(\Phi\).
3765	  \end{enumerate}
3766	  Two pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) and \((\mathcal{F}', q')\) are equivalent iff
3767	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). On morphisms \(f : T' \to T\) over \(S\), the functor
3768	  acts by pulling back the quotient along \(X_{T'} \to X_T\) (well-defined
3769	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
3770	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
3771	  \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\).
3772	\end{definition}
3773	
3774	\subsection{Base-change well-definedness of the Quot functor}
3775	\label{sec:quot_base_change}
3776	
3777	The pullback action of the Quot functor (\cref{def:quot_functor}) along an
3778	\(S\)-morphism \(T' \to T\) is well defined because each of the four
3779	conditions on a family of quotients is preserved by base change; this is the
3780	content of the following four lemmas.  Throughout, write
3781	\(g' : X_{T'} \to X_T\) for the induced morphism on relative products, which
3782	is cartesian over \(T' \to T\).
3783	
3784	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3785	[Pullback preserves finite presentation]
3786	  \label{lem:pullback_finite_presentation}
3787	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation}
3788	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1 (read from
3789	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L418-L419):
3790	  % "Then as properness and flatness are preserved by base-change, and as
3791	  %  tensor-product is right exact, the pull-back of <F, q> ... is
3792	  %  well-defined"; cf. Stacks, Modules, "pullback of finitely presented
3793	  %  modules is finitely presented".
3794	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3795	  Let \(g : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{F}\) a finitely
3796	  presented sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (locally the cokernel of a map
3797	  of finite free modules).  Then \(g^* \mathcal{F}\) is a finitely presented
3798	  sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_Y\)-modules.
3799	\end{lemma}
3800	\begin{proof}
3801	  % NOTE: the Lean proof is complete modulo the index-bookkeeping leaf
3802	  % `Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite` (the transported presentation
3803	  % keeps its index types; the definitional reduction certifying this is
3804	  % recorded as a typed sorry for performance reasons).
3805	  Choose an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on whose members \(\mathcal{F}\)
3806	  admits presentations
3807	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\oplus b_i} \to \mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\oplus a_i} \to
3808	  \mathcal{F}|_{U_i} \to 0\) with \(a_i, b_i\) finite.  The preimages
3809	  \(g^{-1}(U_i)\) cover \(Y\).  The pullback functor is a left adjoint, hence
3810	  right exact, and it sends \(\mathcal{O}_X\) to \(\mathcal{O}_Y\) and finite
3811	  free modules to finite free modules of the same rank; moreover restriction
3812	  to \(g^{-1}(U_i)\) of the pullback is the pullback of the restriction along
3813	  \(g^{-1}(U_i) \to U_i\).  Applying the restricted pullback to the chosen
3814	  presentation therefore yields a presentation
3815	  \(\mathcal{O}^{\oplus b_i} \to \mathcal{O}^{\oplus a_i} \to
3816	  (g^*\mathcal{F})|_{g^{-1}(U_i)} \to 0\) with the same finite index sets.
3817	\end{proof}
3818	
3819	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3820	[Flatness over the base is stable under base change]
3821	  \label{lem:coherent_flat_base_change}
3822	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback}
3823	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
3824	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1 (read from
3825	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L418-L419);
3826	  % cf. Stacks 01U9 (flatness of morphisms is stable under base change).
3827	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); Stacks 01U9.}
3828	  Let
3829	  \[
3830	    \begin{array}{ccc}
3831	      X' & \xrightarrow{\ g'\ } & X \\
3832	      \downarrow f' & & \downarrow f \\
3833	      S' & \xrightarrow{\ g\ } & S
3834	    \end{array}
3835	  \]
3836	  be a cartesian square of schemes and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent sheaf
3837	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules that is flat over \(S\) in the sense of
3838	  \cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}.  Then \(g'^* \mathcal{F}\) is flat over
3839	  \(S'\).
3840	\end{lemma}
3841	\begin{proof}
3842	  For a quasi-coherent module, the affine-pair flatness predicate of
3843	  \cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat} is equivalent to flatness of every stalk
3844	  \(\mathcal{F}_x\) over the local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S, f(x)}\): a module
3845	  over a ring \(B\) with an action of \(A\) through \(A \to B\) is flat over
3846	  \(A\) if and only if all its localizations at primes of \(B\) are flat over
3847	  the corresponding localizations of \(A\).  The stalk of \(g'^*\mathcal{F}\)
3848	  at \(x' \in X'\) is
3849	  \(\mathcal{F}_{g'(x')} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{X, g'(x')}}
3850	  \mathcal{O}_{X', x'}\), and since the square is cartesian
3851	  \(\mathcal{O}_{X', x'}\) is a localization of
3852	  \(\mathcal{O}_{X, g'(x')} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{S, s}}
3853	  \mathcal{O}_{S', s'}\) (where \(s = f(g'(x'))\), \(s' = f'(x')\)).  Hence
3854	  \((g'^*\mathcal{F})_{x'}\) is a localization of
3855	  \(\mathcal{F}_{g'(x')} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{S,s}} \mathcal{O}_{S', s'}\);
3856	  flatness over \(\mathcal{O}_{S,s}\) base-changes to flatness over
3857	  \(\mathcal{O}_{S', s'}\) and is preserved by localization.
3858	\end{proof}
3859	
3860	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3861	[Proper support is stable under base change]
3862	  \label{lem:proper_support_base_change}
3863	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.HasProperSupport.of_isPullback}
3864	  \uses{def:has_proper_support, def:modules_annihilator}
3865	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1 (read from
3866	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L418-L419);
3867	  % cf. Stacks 01W4 (properness is stable under base change) and 056H
3868	  % (support of the pullback of a finite-type module).
3869	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3870	  In the cartesian square of \cref{lem:coherent_flat_base_change}, let
3871	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a finitely presented sheaf of
3872	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose schematic support is proper over \(S\)
3873	  (\cref{def:has_proper_support}).  Then the schematic support of
3874	  \(g'^* \mathcal{F}\) is proper over \(S'\).
3875	\end{lemma}
3876	\begin{proof}
3877	  A local section of \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) pulls back to a section
3878	  annihilating \(g'^* \mathcal{F}\), so the pullback ideal
3879	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F}) \cdot \mathcal{O}_{X'}\) is contained in
3880	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(g'^*\mathcal{F})\) (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}).
3881	  Consequently the schematic support of \(g'^* \mathcal{F}\) is a closed
3882	  subscheme of the base change
3883	  \(Z' := Z \times_X X' = Z \times_S S'\) of the schematic support
3884	  \(Z\) of \(\mathcal{F}\).  Since \(Z \to S\) is proper and properness is
3885	  stable under base change, \(Z' \to S'\) is proper; a closed immersion
3886	  followed by a proper morphism is proper, so the schematic support of
3887	  \(g'^* \mathcal{F}\) is proper over \(S'\).
3888	\end{proof}
3889	
3890	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3891	[The fibrewise Hilbert function is invariant under base change]
3892	  \label{lem:hilbert_fibre_base_change}
3893	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap}
3894	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:has_proper_support}
3895	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
3896	  % Polynomials" (read from
3897	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L498):
3898	  % the decomposition Quot = \coprod_\Phi Quot^\Phi presupposes that the
3899	  % Hilbert polynomial of the fibres is invariant under base change.
3900	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3901	  Let \(\pi : X \to S\) be locally of finite type, \(L\) a sheaf of
3902	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, \(T' \to T\) a morphism of \(S\)-schemes, and
3903	  \(\mathcal{F}\) a finitely presented sheaf on \(X_T\) whose schematic
3904	  support is proper over \(T\).  For \(t' \in T'\) with image \(t \in T\)
3905	  and every \(m \in \mathbb{N}\),
3906	  \[
3907	    \dim_{\kappa(t')} \Gamma\bigl((X_{T'})_{t'},\,
3908	      (g'^*\mathcal{F})_{t'} \otimes L_{t'}^{\otimes m}\bigr)
3909	    \;=\;
3910	    \dim_{\kappa(t)} \Gamma\bigl((X_T)_t,\,
3911	      \mathcal{F}_t \otimes L_t^{\otimes m}\bigr),
3912	  \]
3913	  where \(g' : X_{T'} \to X_T\) is the induced morphism.  In particular the
3914	  graded Hilbert function of \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial} is invariant under
3915	  base change of the parameter scheme.
3916	\end{lemma}
3917	\begin{proof}
3918	  Since \(X_{T'} = X_T \times_T T'\), the fibre of \(X_{T'} \to T'\) at
3919	  \(t'\) is the base change \((X_T)_t \times_{\operatorname{Spec} \kappa(t)}
3920	  \operatorname{Spec} \kappa(t')\) of the fibre at \(t\) along the residue
3921	  field extension \(\kappa(t) \to \kappa(t')\), and under this identification
3922	  \((g'^*\mathcal{F})_{t'} \otimes L_{t'}^{\otimes m}\) is the pullback of
3923	  \(\mathcal{F}_t \otimes L_t^{\otimes m}\).  Sections of a module
3924	  annihilated by the ideal of a closed subscheme are computed on that
3925	  subscheme, so both sides are computed on the support of the twisted
3926	  module, which is proper — in particular quasi-compact and separated — over
3927	  the respective residue field.  Global sections of a quasi-coherent module
3928	  on a qcqs scheme commute with flat base change of the (affine) base; a
3929	  field extension \(\kappa(t) \to \kappa(t')\) is flat, so
3930	  \(\Gamma\) of the base-changed module is
3931	  \(\Gamma\bigl((X_T)_t, \mathcal{F}_t \otimes L_t^{\otimes m}\bigr)
3932	  \otimes_{\kappa(t)} \kappa(t')\).  Finally
3933	  \(\dim_{\kappa(t')} (V \otimes_{\kappa(t)} \kappa(t')) =
3934	  \dim_{\kappa(t)} V\) for any \(\kappa(t)\)-vector space \(V\) (in the
3935	  infinite-dimensional case both dimensions are infinite, and both sides of
3936	  the displayed equality carry the same junk value).
3937	\end{proof}
3938	
3939	\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
3940	\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}
3941	
3942	The Quot construction proceeds by embedding \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) as a
3943	locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over \(S\). Since Mathlib carries
3944	no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
3945	linear algebra), we package the construction here.
3946	
3947	\begin{definition}\leanok
3948	[Grassmannian scheme]
3949	  \label{def:grassmannian_scheme}
3950	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian}
3951	  \uses{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
3952	  % NOTE (run 0010 T12): the Lean definition is now REAL
3953	  % (AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean), encoding the displayed set of
3954	  % rank-d locally free quotients of V_T directly (`Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient`,
3955	  % Setoid quotient, pullback action along \psi.left).  The definition does NOT
3956	  % factor through def:quot_functor: the locally-free encoding is the form of the
3957	  % universal property valid over an arbitrary base, and it is the form matched by
3958	  % the merged chart construction (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`).  The
3959	  % identification with the Quot special case (final sentence below) holds in the
3960	  % locally noetherian regime and is future work, not a definitional dependency.
3961	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises~(2)" and
3962	  % "Construction of Grassmannian" (read from
3963	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L599
3964	  % + L773-L850).
3965	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3966	  % "For any integers \(r\ge d\ge 1\), an explicit construction
3967	  %  the Grassmannian scheme
3968	  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\), together with the tautological
3969	  %  quotient \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) where \
3970	  %  \(\U\) is a rank \(d\) locally free sheaf on \(\grass(r,d)\),
3971	  %  has been given at the end of this section. [...]
3972	  %  Show that \(\grass(r,d)\) together with the quotient
3973	  %  \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) represents the
3974	  %  contravariant functor
3975	  %  \(\)\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}\(\)
3976	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any \(T\) the
3977	  %  set of all equivalence classes
3978	  %  \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients \(q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F\)
3979	  %  where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\).
3980	  %  [...]
3981	  %  Using the above show that, more generally, if \(S\) is a scheme and
3982	  %  \(E\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\),
3983	  %  the functor \(\Grass(E,d)=\Quot_{E/S/S}^{d,{\mathcal O}_S}\)
3984	  %  on all \(S\)-schemes which
3985	  %  by definition associates to any \(T\)
3986	  %  the set of all equivalence classes \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients
3987	  %  \(q: E_T \to \F\) where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\),
3988	  %  is representable. The representing scheme
3989	  %  is denoted by \(\grass(E,d)\) and is called the rank \(d\)
3990	  %  relative Grassmannian of \(E\) over \(S\)."
3991	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) and "Construction of
3992	  Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3993	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(V\) be a locally free
3994	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\). For an integer \(1 \le d \le r\), the
3995	  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-\(d\) quotients of \(V\)} is the functor
3996	  \[
3997	    \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set},
3998	    \qquad
3999	    T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle :
4000	    q : V_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F},\; \mathcal{F}
4001	    \text{ locally free of rank } d \}
4002	  \]
4003	  with equivalence \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\); the rank-\(d\) local freeness of
4004	  \(\mathcal{F}\) is as in \cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}. Over a locally
4005	  noetherian base this functor agrees with the Quot functor
4006	  (\cref{def:quot_functor}) in the special case \(X = S\), \(E = V\), constant
4007	  Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\): a flat finitely presented module whose fibre
4008	  dimension is \(d\) at every point is locally free of rank \(d\).
4009	\end{definition}
